seoTitle: Jabuk vs Jamestown — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Jabuk and Jamestown. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jabuk and Jamestown.
heading: Jabuk vs Jamestown — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jabuk, South Australia and Jamestown, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jamestown has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Jabuk (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jabuk or Jamestown safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jabuk has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Jamestown scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jabuk scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Jamestown scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jabuk or Jamestown?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jamestown has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
